buggy_code:
class Counter:
    count = 0
    def increment(self):
        self.count += 1

a = Counter()
b = Counter()
a.increment()
print(b.count)
task: Each Counter instance should track its own count independently
error_location: line 2: count defined as class attribute, shared until instance shadow
clarification_query: How should count be defined so each instance has its own independent counter?
explanation: Class attributes are shared. self.count += 1 creates an instance attribute on a, but b.count still reads the class attribute. Initializing in __init__ ensures independence.
